explicit_text: I was born in Quebec, a suburb of Quebec City, and grew up there until I was 22. It was a Catholic environment, but I became an atheist at 12 and developed a strong interest in science and evolution while living there. Quebec City is where my early life and foundational beliefs took shape before I embarked on my journey into Raëlism and other pursuits.

explicit_text: I discovered Raëlism in 1976 through an article, and it completely changed my worldview. The idea that life on Earth was scientifically created by extraterrestrial beings fascinated me, especially since it aligned with my love for mysteries and science. Despite opposition from my family and friends, I embraced Raëlism for over 45 years and now serve as a Raelian representative for the West Coast, including Alaska and Hawaii.

explicit_text: The COVID-19 pandemic was a disappointing time for me because politics overshadowed science and many specialists were silenced. I was critical of the rushed vaccine development and the suppression of alternative treatments like hydroxychloroquine and ivermectin. The pandemic revealed to me the deep corruption and control by pharmaceutical companies and governments, which was very troubling.

explicit_text: Hydroxychloroquine was vilified unfairly during the pandemic despite being a common and well-known medication. I saw false articles published in respected journals like Nature that discredited it, which I believe was part of a plan to clear the way for vaccines. The suppression of hydroxychloroquine was, in my opinion, driven by financial and political motives rather than science.
